canonical_definition: Across the surveyed literature, language modeling is predominantly defined as the observable behavior of predicting the next unit in a sequence given the preceding context, with variations specifying this unit as a token, word, symbol, or byte. A secondary framing, found in papers like "From Tokens to Lattices", treats it as the task of predicting masked tokens from bidirectional context. A smaller set of papers conceptualizes it more abstractly as a latent "language modeling ability" or "capability" to generate plausible text. This behavior is most commonly operationalized by measuring perplexity on standard text corpora, with WikiText-2, WikiText-103, and C4 appearing as the most frequent evaluation benchmarks. Performance is also widely assessed through zero-shot accuracy on downstream tasks, including LAMBADA, MMLU, HellaSwag, and WinoGrande. The provided data also details more specific variants, such as "In-context n-gram prediction" and "Audio token prediction". Language modeling is studied alongside text generation, with one paper framing it as the process of "aligning model distribution with that of human language" (EMO: EARTH MOVER DISTANCE OPTIMIZATION FOR AUTO-REGRESSIVE LANGUAGE MODELING), and is also connected to long-context processing and factual recall.
expert_removed_reason: method/engineering — not a construct/measurement/behavior of LLM ability

definitions.text: Predicting or scoring text sequences as a language model, as reflected here by perplexity evaluation.
definitions.paper: ESPACE: Dimensionality Reduction of Activations for Model Compression

definitions.text: The fundamental task of a Masked Language Model, which involves predicting a masked token based on its surrounding bidirectional context.
definitions.paper: From Tokens to Lattices: Emergent Lattice Structures in Language Models

definitions.text: The observable quality of the model's language modeling capabilities during the initial pre-training phase.
definitions.paper: Mix-LN: Unleashing the Power of Deeper Layers by Combining Pre-LN and Post-LN

definitions.text: The process of generating a sequence of tokens one at a time, where each new token is conditioned on the sequence of previously generated tokens.
definitions.paper: Flash Inference: Near Linear Time Inference for Long Convolution Sequence Models and Beyond

definitions.text: Predicting original tokens at positions that were randomly masked in the input sequence.
definitions.paper: Masked Diffusion Models are Secretly Time-Agnostic Masked Models and Exploit Inaccurate Categorical Sampling

definitions.text: Using prior tokens in a sequence to estimate the next state when the sequence follows a recursive dependence on the previous state.
definitions.paper: Towards Understanding the Universality of Transformers for Next-Token Prediction

definitions.text: The task of predicting the next token in a sequence given the preceding tokens.
definitions.paper: Understanding Factual Recall in Transformers via Associative Memories

definitions.text: The task of predicting the next token in a sequence given the preceding tokens.
definitions.paper: How new data permeates LLM knowledge and how to dilute it

definitions.text: The observable task of predicting, for each position in a sequence, the set of all valid symbols that could legally appear next according to the rules of a given formal language.
definitions.paper: Training Neural Networks as Recognizers of Formal Languages

definitions.text: Predicting discrete audio-token sequences from text and/or prompt audio in an autoregressive or non-autoregressive manner.
definitions.paper: HALL-E: Hierarchical Neural Codec Language Model for Minute-Long Zero-Shot Text-to-Speech Synthesis

definitions.text: The task of predicting the next byte in a sequence given the preceding bytes.
definitions.paper: Exact Byte-Level Probabilities from Tokenized Language Models for FIM-Tasks and Model Ensembles

definitions.text: Producing the next token from intermediate or final hidden states during language modeling or early-exit inference.
definitions.paper: Relaxed Recursive Transformers: Effective Parameter Sharing with Layer-wise LoRA

definitions.text: The task of predicting the next token in a sequence based on an n-gram language model derived from the context of that same sequence.
definitions.paper: Learning In-context $n$-grams with Transformers: Sub-$n$-grams Are Near-Stationary Points

definitions.text: The observable behavior of predicting the next token based solely on its overall frequency in the sequence, ignoring contextual dependencies.
definitions.paper: Learning In-context $n$-grams with Transformers: Sub-$n$-grams Are Near-Stationary Points

definitions.text: The observable behavior of predicting the next token based on the immediately preceding token, capturing first-order sequential dependencies.
definitions.paper: Learning In-context $n$-grams with Transformers: Sub-$n$-grams Are Near-Stationary Points

definitions.text: The observable behavior of predicting the next token based on the two preceding tokens, capturing second-order sequential dependencies.
definitions.paper: Learning In-context $n$-grams with Transformers: Sub-$n$-grams Are Near-Stationary Points

definitions.text: A training task where a model performs next-token prediction on a sequence from which a random subset of past tokens has been dropped or masked.
definitions.paper: Generative Audio Language Modeling with Continuous-valued Tokens and Masked Next-Token Prediction

definitions.text: The observable behavior of predicting a missing word in a sentence, either the final word or a randomly selected non-final word, based on context.
definitions.paper: Simple Yet Effective: An Information-Theoretic Approach to Multi-LLMUncertainty Quantification

definitions.text: The overall ability of a language model to make accurate predictions, as measured by metrics like perplexity and zero-shot task accuracy.
definitions.paper: SpQR: A Sparse-Quantized Representation for Near-Lossless LLM Weight Compression

definitions.text: The model's core capability to understand and generate plausible, meaningful text, which should be preserved during debiasing interventions.
definitions.paper: Rethinking Channel Dimensions to Isolate Outliers for Low-bit Weight Quantization of Large Language Models

definitions.text: The latent ability of a model to accurately predict the next symbol in a sequence based on prior context, reflecting its underlying probabilistic modeling strength.
definitions.paper: Language Modeling Is Compression

definitions.text: The fundamental ability of a model to understand and generate plausible sequences of text, which can be recovered more easily than factual knowledge during fine-tuning.
definitions.paper: AmoebaLLM: Constructing Any-Shape Large Language Models for Efficient and Instant Deployment

definitions.text: The latent ability of the model to predict and generate coherent language sequences, often inferred from probability distributions over tokens.
definitions.paper: Accuracy is Not All You Need

definitions.text: The overall proficiency of a model in predicting the next token in a sequence, measured across various standard language benchmarks.
definitions.paper: Selective Attention: Enhancing Transformer through Principled Context Control

definitions.text: The latent ability of the decoder to understand and represent the source language effectively, whether derived from speech or text, which supports improved translation and transcription.
definitions.paper: Demystifying optimized prompts in language models

relationships.paper: ProbingLLMWorld Models: Enhancing Guesstimation with Wisdom of Crowds Decoding

emnlp25: ProbingLLMWorld Models: Enhancing Guesstimation with Wisdom of Crowds Decoding

characterization: Across the surveyed literature, language modeling is predominantly defined as the observable behavior of predicting the next unit in a sequence given the preceding context, with variations specifying this unit as a token, word, symbol, or byte. A secondary framing, found in papers like "From Tokens to Lattices", treats it as the task of predicting masked tokens from bidirectional context. A smaller set of papers conceptualizes it more abstractly as a latent "language modeling ability" or "capability" to generate plausible text. This behavior is most commonly operationalized by measuring perplexity on standard text corpora, with WikiText-2, WikiText-103, and C4 appearing as the most frequent evaluation benchmarks. Performance is also widely assessed through zero-shot accuracy on downstream tasks, including LAMBADA, MMLU, HellaSwag, and WinoGrande. The provided data also details more specific variants, such as "In-context n-gram prediction" and "Audio token prediction". Language modeling is studied alongside text generation, with one paper framing it as the process of "aligning model distribution with that of human language" (EMO: EARTH MOVER DISTANCE OPTIMIZATION FOR AUTO-REGRESSIVE LANGUAGE MODELING), and is also connected to long-context processing and factual recall.
